name: Zyra
playstyleSummary: Zyra is a mage who fights with an army of living plants as much as with her own spells. She feels like a trap-setter: you plant seeds ahead of time, then detonate Deadly Spines or Grasping Roots near them to grow turret-like plants that keep fighting even while you do something else, all backed by heavy area damage and a knockup ultimate.
strengths: Plants provide extra damage and vision without Zyra having to be present
Grasping Roots can root a target, setting up big damage or a kill for her team
Stranglethorns deals big area damage and knocks enemies airborne, great for starting or winning teamfights
Strong at controlling an area — enemies who walk in take damage from spells, plants, and roots all at once
weaknesses: Very fragile and easy to kill if caught without her crowd control up
Needs to preposition seeds ahead of time to get full value from her kit, which takes planning
Plants can be destroyed simply by stepping on the seed before it grows
Loses a lot of value if the fight moves away from her seeded area

abilities.name: Garden of Thorns
abilities.howToUseIt: Seeds spawn near you automatically over time. Cast Deadly Spines or Grasping Roots close to a seed to instantly grow a plant that fights alongside you — try to always have seeds down before a fight starts.

abilities.name: Deadly Spines
abilities.howToUseIt: Use this to damage a group of enemies, or cast it right on top of a seed to grow a Thorn Spitter plant that keeps attacking enemies from range even after you move away.

abilities.name: Rampant Growth
abilities.howToUseIt: This plants a seed for later use — drop seeds in brush or along paths enemies are likely to walk through, since you can hold several seeds at once and killing enemies lets you plant more, faster.

abilities.name: Grasping Roots
abilities.howToUseIt: This roots the first enemy it hits, so use it to lock down a target before following up with damage, or cast it near a seed to grow a Vine Lasher that slows enemies who get close to it.

abilities.name: Stranglethorns
abilities.howToUseIt: Cast this on a grouped-up enemy team — it deals damage as it expands and then knocks everyone inside airborne as it closes, making it a strong teamfight-starting or teamfight-winning tool, especially with plants already enraged nearby.

earlyGamePlan: In lane, plant seeds with Rampant Growth in spots you expect fights to happen, like brush near the wave. Use Deadly Spines to poke and farm minions, and look for a Grasping Roots root on an overextended enemy to set up a kill with your ally. Be cautious engaging without a seed nearby, since a plant can make a fight go your way.
quickTips: Keep seeds down in brush or narrow paths before a fight starts, not during it
Grow a plant right before a fight so it can tank hits and deal damage while you stay safer
Land Grasping Roots first in a trade — it sets up everything else you do
Save Stranglethorns for when the enemy team is grouped up so the knockup hits multiple people
Watch your own health — you have low defenses and can be killed quickly if caught without crowd control ready
Destroy enemy seeds by walking over them if you see one placed near you
whatToExpect: An enemy Zyra will try to seed the area around her before a fight, then grow plants and land Grasping Roots on you to set up damage or a kill — avoid standing near unclaimed seeds so she can't turn them into plants for free. She's very fragile if you catch her without her root or a plant nearby, and her ultimate can knock your whole team airborne if you group up on top of her seeds, so consider spreading out before engaging.

glossary.definition: Ability effects like roots, stuns, or knockups that limit an enemy's ability to move or act.
A crowd control effect that stops an enemy from moving, though they can usually still attack or cast spells.
A crowd control effect that launches an enemy into the air, briefly preventing them from acting.
Chipping away at an enemy's health from a safe distance before a real fight starts.
Earning gold and experience by last-hitting minions.
Patches of tall grass on the map that hide anyone standing inside from enemies outside it.
A fight where most or all champions from both teams are involved at once.
Pushing too far into dangerous territory, past the point where your team can help you.
